## Agent Workbench Managed Block

This block is managed by `scripts/render-agent-workbench.py project-docs`. Preserve project-authored instructions outside these markers.

### Shared Rules

- Follow Agent Workbench branch safety before modifying files.
- Check project docs and manifests before assuming commands or stack.
- Keep project-specific lessons in this repo's local docs when they are durable.
- Do not treat this repo as the personal workbench source unless it is `projects/agent-workbench`; `claude-config` is only a legacy compatibility alias.

### Major Project Protocol

- Treat this as a major coding project when it spans multiple sessions, exceeds 2M tokens, crosses roughly $10 estimated spend, involves infra/auth/data risk, or needs a living handoff.
- For major-project work, keep `PLAN.md`, `docs/BUILD-LOG.md`, or `docs/next-session-prompt.md` current enough for a fresh session to resume.
- For controller-session setup, inspect repo context first, use grill-me preflight when it materially reduces later interruption, group blockers and likely questions up front, and record known blockers, ask-before items, approved actions, validation gates, and stop conditions in `docs/next-session-prompt.md`.
- Use subagents or parallel tool calls for bounded read-only exploration, review, test diagnosis, docs drift, and validation planning when available; keep one lead responsible for decisions, integration, and final claims.
- Refresh the handoff at phase changes, and start a fresh Codex session when switching from audit to implementation, changing workstreams, or after a large verification/debug loop.
- For sessions from a plan or expected to take more than 10 minutes, send compact Workbench progress cards at most once per 10-point progress bucket using a real hour and minute like `**🟨 30% · 2026-06-04 16:03 Toronto**` plus Done/Next/ETA bullets; date-only Toronto is invalid. Use the matching blocked card when progress is blocked.
- For browser UI, OAuth/auth redirects, connect/disconnect flows, embedded previews, clipboard/upload/browser compatibility, or any click-visible workflow, code tests and API checks are necessary but not sufficient; attempt Browser/Chrome automation, screenshot, DOM inspection, network/URL-shape evidence, or exact user-operated browser steps. If unavailable, record blocked/not tested and do not claim browser/user-facing live interaction passed.
- Final answers and handoffs must distinguish local tests passed, server/API live checks passed, browser/user-facing live interaction passed, and blocked/not tested.
- Regression example: `/api/connect/sessions` returning `200`, host, expiry, and token length does not prove the browser-visible OAuth/Nango modal works.

### Discovered Facts

- `package.json` exists; inspect scripts before running commands.
- `README.md` exists; read it before broad exploration.
